# Issues Manifest

> This issues manifest was generated by the analyst agent. All issues require triage by the lead agent before they enter the implementation pipeline.

## Schema

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `ID` | Unique identifier `ISS-NNN`, assigned sequentially after final severity sort. Stable after assignment — never renumber. |
| `Severity` | `critical` \| `major` \| `minor` |
| `Category` | `security` \| `quality` \| `convention` \| `testing` \| `dependency` \| `documentation` \| `build` \| `functionality` |
| `Source` | One or more source artifact paths, comma-separated (`specs/.analysis/1.*.md`, `specs/prd.md`, `specs/features/*.md`, `specs/adr/*.md`, `specs/threat-model.md`). |
| `Location` | Code location: file path and line range where the issue was observed. |
| `Description` | What the issue is. |
| `Rationale` | Why it matters (impact, risk, deviation from best practice). |
| `Triage` | `pending` (initial) \| `promote` \| `new-frd` \| `needs-investigation` \| `accepted-debt` \| `duplicate`. Set by the lead agent during triage. |
| `Resolution` | Free-text note explaining the triage decision (e.g., target FRD, proposed FRD title, debt rationale, duplicate reference). Initialized blank by the analyst; populated by the lead agent during triage. |

## Issues

Sort order: `Severity` (critical → major → minor), then insertion order within each severity group. IDs are assigned sequentially after sorting and are stable — never renumber after assignment.

| ID | Severity | Category | Source | Location | Description | Rationale | Triage | Resolution |
|----|----------|----------|--------|----------|-------------|-----------|--------|------------|
